refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall update the worksite model within the customer-required timeliness for operational use.
refined_fr.rationale: The candidate FR specifies a single functional obligation: timely updating of the worksite model according to customer requirements. It does not combine multiple functions or include implementation details, thus it is atomic and solution-neutral.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure the latency of worksite model updates under supported scenarios and verify that the 95th percentile latency (p95) meets or is below the customer-defined target (e.g., ≤ 1.0 second).

candidate_dps: Incremental model update mechanism triggered by detected changes
Periodic full model refresh at fixed intervals
Event-driven update process based on external triggers
Hybrid approach combining incremental updates with periodic full refreshes
selected_dp.selected_dp: Incremental model update mechanism triggered by detected changes

potential_sources: Synchronization between update process and data sources
coupling_check.notes: The incremental update approach isolates the update logic to changes only, reducing coupling with full model refresh processes and supporting timeliness requirements.

assumptions_risks: Change detection is reliable and timely to trigger updates
Incremental updates can be processed within the latency target
Complexity of incremental updates does not introduce errors or inconsistencies
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Clarify the nature and frequency of update scenarios to better tailor the DP selection and ensure alignment with latency targets.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: The NGHE Solution module encompasses the core system logic responsible for managing model updates and integration with other system components.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Receives data inputs that may trigger model updates.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Sends updated model tasks to other modules.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Facilitates communication for update propagation and synchronization.

module_containment_scores.notes: High cohesion as the incremental model update mechanism is central to NGHE Solution; minimal diffusion outside this module.
Partial containment possible but would require significant diffusion to other modules for update triggers and propagation.
Low containment; teleoperation focuses on control rather than model updates, causing high diffusion.
Very low containment; safety services are peripheral to model update logic, leading to high diffusion.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The incremental model update mechanism is best contained within the NGHE Solution module, which centralizes core system logic and minimizes diffusion across other modules.
containment_risks: Potential coupling with Telemetry and Tasking interfaces may require careful interface management to avoid tight coupling.
If model updates require real-time safety checks, some diffusion to Safety Services might occur.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: If containment risks increase, consider refining the DP to explicitly separate update triggering from update application to better modularize responsibilities within NGHE Solution.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The DP 'Incremental model update mechanism triggered by detected changes' directly addresses the FR of updating the worksite model within the required timeliness by focusing on incremental updates triggered by changes, which supports meeting the latency acceptance criteria.

diffusion_risks: Potential coupling with Telemetry and Tasking interfaces may require careful interface management to avoid tight coupling.
If model updates require real-time safety checks, some diffusion to Safety Services might occur.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: The NGHE Solution module shows high containment (score 8) and low diffusion (score 2), indicating that the DP is well contained within a single IM boundary. Other modules show lower containment and higher diffusion, supporting the choice of NGHE Solution as the ownership candidate.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification of the FR through the IM is feasible by measuring the update latency (p95 ≤ 1.0 s) and ensuring the NGHE Solution module correctly interfaces with Telemetry, Tasking, and Network Link as specified.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: The allocation of the DP to the NGHE Solution IM is clear and well justified, with explicit interfaces defined for data input, task output, and communication, supporting traceability from FR to IM.

recommended_revisions: Monitor interface coupling with Telemetry and Tasking to avoid tight coupling that could increase diffusion risk.
If safety-related update requirements grow, consider refining the DP to separate update triggering from update application to modularize responsibilities within NGHE Solution.
Regularly reassess containment scores to ensure the DP remains well contained as system complexity evolves.

decision.justification: All gates (FR->DP, DP->IM, FR->IM) have passed with clear justification and feasible verification. The DP is well contained within a single IM boundary with manageable diffusion risks. The high safety criticality has been considered, and recommended revisions focus on monitoring and reassessment rather than fundamental changes. Therefore, no further decomposition into sub-FRs is necessary at this time.

revise.why: Monitor interface coupling with Telemetry and Tasking to avoid tight coupling and reassess containment scores regularly to maintain low diffusion risk as system complexity evolves.

assumptions: The current DP sufficiently addresses the FR with incremental updates triggered by detected changes.
The NGHE Solution module is the appropriate IM for implementing the DP with high containment and low diffusion.
Verification of update latency and interface correctness is feasible and planned.
open_risks: Potential coupling with Telemetry and Tasking interfaces may increase diffusion risk if not managed.
Possible diffusion to Safety Services if real-time safety checks become necessary.
System complexity growth may affect containment and diffusion scores over time.
evidence_needed: Interface interaction logs between NGHE Solution and Telemetry, Tasking, Network Link modules.
Containment and diffusion score reassessments as system evolves.
